title: Course Flow
description: An interactive prerequisite graph for UCSD students to visualize and plan their entire academic path, running entirely client-side. Built with React, TypeScript, React Flow, D3.js, Tailwind CSS, Vite, and Playwright.

title: PAN: A World Model for General, Interactable, and Long-Horizon World Simulation
description: Technical report on PAN, a world model for general, interactable, and long-horizon simulation. Co-authored as a member of Professor Zhiting Hu's lab at UC San Diego.

title: Autonomous Aerial Vision System
description: Vision stack for autonomous aircrafts to identify targets, map terrain, and make decisions without human intervention. Built with C++, YOLOv11, ONNX Runtime, OpenCV, Jetson Orin Nano, and ArduPilot SITL for Triton UAS.

title: Self-Fed Memory: A "Second Brain" AI
description: A personalized RAG assistant that ingests notes and conversations to act as a "Second Brain" with perfect recall. Built with FastAPI, LangChain, Pinecone, Docker, Streamlit, and Supabase.

title: Disinformation Detection: Benchmarking Deep Learning Models
description: Comparative benchmarking of LSTMs vs. Transformers for fake news detection, highlighting generalization gaps across datasets. Built with PyTorch, LSTM architectures, and GloVe embeddings.

title: File Order Randomizer
description: A native extension for Adobe Premiere Pro that randomizes clip order, solving a major workflow bottleneck for video editors. Built with Adobe CEP, ExtendScript, HTML, and CSS.

title: DSC 106 Lab 4: JavaScript II
description: Refactored the portfolio to template projects from a JSON data file using modular ES functions (fetchJSON, renderProjects), displayed the latest three projects on the homepage, and pulled live GitHub profile stats from the GitHub REST API.

title: DSC 106 Lab 3: Introduction to JS
description: Added dynamic JavaScript to the portfolio. Automatic current-page highlighting, a generated navigation bar, a light/dark/automatic theme switcher with persistence, and a working mailto contact form.

title: DSC 106 Lab 2: Styling with CSS
description: Styled the portfolio with Flexbox navigation, a CSS Grid contact form using subgrid, a responsive projects grid with auto-fill columns, and an oklch-based accent color system.

title: DSC 106 Lab 1: Introduction to the Web Platform
description: Built the initial multi-page portfolio scaffold with semantic HTML, linked stylesheets, a shared navigation menu, a basic contact form, and a CV/resume page.

title: Data Science Intern @ Lanner Inc.
description: Built internal data pipelines and analytics dashboards during a summer internship at Lanner Inc., focused on turning raw operational data into actionable product insights.

title: Web Developer Intern @ Silverline Educational Advisory
description: Designed and shipped marketing and admissions pages for an education consulting firm, improving page performance and lead conversion across the main site.
